title: Security
description: Manage users, groups, roles, and API keys

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage user accounts and group memberships
users.stackScopeHint: Select one or more stacks. Leave all unchecked to apply the role to the whole environment.
users.noStacksInEnvironment: No stacks are available in this environment yet.
users.noStacksMatch: No stacks match the current filter.
users.roleAlreadyAssignedGlobal: This role is already assigned globally.
users.roleAlreadyAssignedEnvironment: This role is already assigned to the whole environment.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Organize users into groups with shared permissions
groups.name: Name
groups.deleteGroupConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this group? All member and role assignments will be removed.
groups.noRoles: No roles assigned to this group
groups.systemGroupLocked: System groups cannot be renamed or deleted

roles.title: Roles
roles.name: Name
roles.deleteRoleConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this role? Users with this role will lose the associated permissions.
roles.fullAccessDescription: This role grants unrestricted access to all resources and settings

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Create API keys for programmatic access
apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.revokeKeyConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key? It will no longer be usable.
apiKeys.keyWarning: Make sure to copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again.

scanning.title: Vulnerability Scanners
scanning.description: Configure which vulnerability scanners are available and set default behavior.
scanning.trivyDescription: Open-source vulnerability scanner by Aqua Security
scanning.clairDescription: Server-based vulnerability scanner (requires external Clair v4 server)
scanning.autoScanDescription: Automatically scan container images for vulnerabilities during lifecycle events.
scanning.scanOnInstallDescription: Automatically scan images when installing apps from the App Store
scanning.scanOnUpdateDescription: Automatically scan images when updating installed apps

identity.title: Identity Providers
identity.description: Configure single sign-on with external identity providers
identity.noProvidersDescription: Add an identity provider to enable single sign-on for your users.
identity.selectTypeDescription: Choose which identity provider you want to configure.
identity.name: Display Name
identity.autoProvisionDescription: Automatically create accounts for new SSO users
identity.autoImportGroupsDescription: Automatically create McHarbor groups from provider group claims and sync membership on each login
identity.fetchGroupsDescription: Fetch groups from the identity provider API to pre-populate mappings
identity.groupMappingDescription: Manually map specific provider groups to existing McHarbor groups
identity.groupMappingHint: When a user signs in via SSO, their provider group memberships are matched against these mappings and the corresponding McHarbor group memberships are applied.
identity.callbackUrlHint: Use this URL as the redirect URI in your provider configuration

entraId.title: Entra ID Setup Guide
entraId.step1Description: Go to the Azure Portal → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations → New registration. Enter a name (e.g. "McHarbor SSO") and select "Accounts in this organizational directory only".
entraId.step2Description: Under Redirect URI, select "Web" and enter the callback URL shown below. Click Register.
entraId.step3Description: Go to Certificates & secrets → Client secrets → New client secret. Add a description and choose an expiry. Copy the secret Value (not the Secret ID).
entraId.step4Description: On the Overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and the Directory (tenant) ID. You will need these for the configuration below.
entraId.step5Description: Go to API permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Delegated permissions. Add: openid, profile, email. If you want group claims, also add GroupMember.Read.All and enable "Emit groups as role claims" in Token configuration.

google.title: Google Workspace Setup Guide
google.step1Description: Go to the Google Cloud Console → Create a new project or select an existing one.
google.step2Description: Go to APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen. Select "Internal" for Workspace users only, or "External" for all Google accounts. Fill in the app name and authorized domains.
google.step3Description: Go to APIs & Services → Credentials → Create Credentials → OAuth client ID. Select "Web application" and enter the callback URL shown below as an authorized redirect URI.
google.step4Description: Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the credentials page. You will need these for the configuration below.
google.step5Description: If you want group mapping, enable the Admin SDK API under APIs & Services → Library. This allows McHarbor to read group memberships from your Workspace directory.

identity.deleteProviderConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this identity provider? Users who signed in through this provider will no longer be able to use SSO.
